---
title: "OGC GERS discussion"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-03-05 23:17"
image: "gers.jpg"
description: "The #OGC seeking public comments on whether the #GERS (Global Entity Reference System) Framework and Model by the #OvertureMapsFoundation (#OMF) should become an OGC Community Standard sparks a broader debate about global identifiers and governance of key data  infrastructure assets."
---

The Open Geospatial Consortium[^OGC] [is seeking comments][comments] on 
"Justification for the Global Entity Reference System (GERS)[^GERS] Framework 
and Model" as an [OGC Community Standard][ogc-standard]. The GERS Model and 
Framework ([previously][gers]) was submitted by the Overture Maps Foundation[^OMF].
The justification describes the rationale for defining and using a Global 
Entity Reference System and its suitability for adoption as an OGC Community 
Standard. The comment period was first announced to end on March 3. However, the 
[GitHub repository for the discussion][github] is still open and at least one 
comment has been added as late as yesterday (March 4).

![GERS: Global Entity Reference System (source: OMF)](gers.jpg "GERS: Global Entity Reference System (source: OMF)")

The Justification document can be found [here][justification] (docx). In the 
OGC repository, there is an [interesting comment by the OpenStreetMap 
Foundation (OSMF)][osmf][^OSMF] that is worth reading, among others. On the 
[OpenStreetMap community forum][forum], there is [a broader discussion and 
debate about OMF and its relationship to OSM and the OSMF][discussion]. That 
thread spans a period of more than two years. In light of the OGC GERS 
discussion, it has seen some recent activity and has also recently been shared 
in social networks by OSM proponents.

Finally, today Bill Dollins weighed in with an article [*"The siren song of 
global identifiers"*][article] that draws analogies to similar discussions in 
the past: 

> Seen in isolation, this debate might appear unique to geospatial data. In 
reality, it follows a pattern that has appeared many times in other technical 
domains. Whenever a global identifier system is proposed, the discussion tends 
to revolve around the same questions: who governs the identifiers, how neutral 
the system is, and whether standardization implicitly elevates one 
implementation over others.

If you only read one thing about the GERS discussion, I recommend [Bill's 
article][article]. It provides a solid overview of the issues at stake and 
"zooms out"^[Often a helpful move.] a bit:

> Ultimately, I think all three participants in this discussion (OGC, Overture, 
and OSMF) are correct. Overture is correct that a universal identifier like 
GERS would be useful. OGC is correct that such a thing should be considered as 
a standard. OSMF is correct that one identifier to rule them all is not 
practical. Each is approaching this issue in good faith, without hyperbole and 
without the “culture war” framing that can sometimes overtake these discussions. 

The [article][article] goes on and ends on an optimistic note.

[^OGC]: Open Geospatial Consortium.
[^GERS]: The [Global Entity Reference System](https://www.spatialists.ch/posts/2025/06/28-omf-launches-gers/) by the Overture Maps Foundation (OMF).
[^OMF]: The [Overture Maps Foundation](https://overturemaps.org/) is an industry consortium (members include Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and TomTom) that produces openly-licensed map data, building primarily on OpenStreetMap data.
[^OSMF]: The [OpenStreetMap Foundation](https://osmfoundation.org/) is a non-profit organisation supporting the OpenStreetMap project.
[^OSM]: [OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/), a collaborative, open-licensed map of the world.

[comments]: https://www.ogc.org/requests/ogc-seeks-public-comment-on-justification-for-the-global-entity-reference-system-gers-framework-and-model-as-an-ogc-community-standard/
[github]: https://github.com/opengeospatial/requests/issues/3
[osmf]: https://github.com/opengeospatial/requests/issues/3#issuecomment-3988672794
[ogc-standard]: https://www.ogc.org/standards-overview/
[forum]: https://community.openstreetmap.org/
[discussion]: https://community.openstreetmap.org/t/overturemaps-org-big-businesses-osmf-alternative/6760/304
[article]: https://blog.geomusings.com/2026/03/05/the-siren-song-of-global-identifiers/
[gers]: https://www.spatialists.ch/posts/2025/06/28-omf-launches-gers/
[justification]: https://portal.ogc.org/files/?artifact_id=112161&version=1
